title: Paneflow configuration
description: Schema for paneflow.json. Lives at ~/.config/paneflow/paneflow.json on Linux, ~/Library/Application Support/paneflow/paneflow.json on macOS, and %APPDATA%\paneflow\paneflow.json on Windows. The runtime is lenient: unknown top-level keys are ignored, so the schema's `additionalProperties: false` is an editor-side aid, not a runtime constraint.

$schema.description: Pointer to this schema. Used by editors (VS Code, Zed, JetBrains) for autocomplete and validation. Ignored at runtime.

$schemaVersion.description: Schema version pin. Paneflow logs a warning when the value is unknown but never refuses to load the file.

default_shell.description: Shell binary path. When null or omitted, falls back to $SHELL, then /bin/sh on Unix, then %COMSPEC% on Windows.

font_family.description: Font family. Accepts the special aliases '.PaneflowMono' (bundled Lilex) and '.PaneflowSans' (bundled IBM Plex Sans), the concrete embedded names ('Lilex', 'IBM Plex Sans'), or any installed system monospace family. Unknown families fall back to Lilex with a warning.

font_fallbacks.description: Ordered fallback font families consulted (in order) for glyphs the primary font_family does not cover — e.g. a Nerd Font for the Powerline/icon glyphs used by Starship, oh-my-posh or Terminal-Icons, which no system font provides on Windows. Null or an empty list keeps GPUI's built-in fallback stack only.

window_decorations.description: Window decoration mode. 'client' = client-side decorations (custom title bar). 'server' = server-side decorations (compositor-drawn). Read once at startup; changes require a restart.

option_as_meta.description: Treat Alt/Option as Meta and send an ESC prefix. Default true on Linux and Windows. On macOS, set to false if you rely on Option to produce Unicode characters.

shell_integration.description: Master switch for the per-shell rc injection (OSC 7 CWD reporting + OSC 133 command marks). null/true = enabled (default); false = no snippet is written or wired - the shell starts exactly as it would outside Paneflow.

agent_stall_detection.description: Master switch for Stalled agent detection. null/true = enabled (default): a Thinking agent session with no hook activity past agent_stall_threshold_secs is flagged Stalled, with ONE desktop notification per stall episode (the flag clears on the next hook event, so a legitimately long turn costs at most one notification). false = kill switch - no Stalled state is ever produced.

agent_stall_threshold_secs.description: Silence threshold in seconds before a Thinking agent session is flagged Stalled (a likely-lost ai.stop). Checked by a 30 s sweep, so the effective detection latency is threshold + up to 30 s. Out-of-range values are clamped.

review_prefill_delay_ms.description: Delay in milliseconds before the Review view types the review prompt into a freshly launched CLI. The prompt is always copied to the clipboard as a fallback, so raise this only if a slow-starting CLI keeps missing the auto-fill. Out-of-range values are clamped.

external_editor.description: Editor used to open file links. 'auto' probes zed, cursor, windsurf, then code; 'system' always uses the OS opener.

shortcuts.description: Custom keybindings overriding the defaults. Format: { 'ctrl+t': 'new_tab' }. The full list of action names is defined in src-app/src/app/actions.rs.

ligatures.description: Render programming-font ligatures (FiraCode '=>', '!=', JetBrains Mono, Cascadia Code). Hot-reloaded.

scrollback_lines.description: Maximum scrollback history in lines. Applied to newly-created terminals.

cursor_shape.description: Default cursor shape before an application overrides it.

env.description: Environment variables injected into every new terminal. Per-surface values win on collision.

scroll_multiplier.description: Scroll-wheel multiplier outside mouse-reporting and alternate-screen modes.

commands.description: cmux-compatible command and workspace definitions, rendered in the workspace command palette.

claude_code_bypass_permissions.description: When true, the built-in 'Claude Code' tab-bar button launches the CLI with --permission-mode bypassPermissions, disabling all permission prompts. Per Anthropic's docs this mode offers no protection against prompt injection — opt in only on machines where that risk is acceptable.

ai_unrestricted.description: AI free-access mode. When true, a conductor (a CLI agent or external orchestrator) may auto-submit prompts to other panes (surface.send_text submit:true) WITHOUT the PANEFLOW_IPC_SCRIPTING env gate, and every such write is traced. Null/false (default) keeps the current behavior strictly unchanged. Re-evaluated per call, so toggling it off revokes the capability with no residual access.

ai_injection_fence.description: Anti-injection fence on the surface.read CLI/IPC path, independent of ai_unrestricted. Null/true (default) wraps returned terminal text in an <untrusted_terminal_output> marker so a malicious peer pane cannot hijack a conductor reading it. False returns raw text (an assumed risk). Stays ON by default even in free-access mode because the fence protects the AI, it does not bridle it.

claude_code_button_visible.description: Show the built-in 'Claude Code' command button. Null/omitted auto-detects the CLI binary.

codex_button_visible.description: Show the built-in 'Codex' command button. Null/omitted auto-detects the CLI binary.

opencode_button_visible.description: Show the built-in 'Opencode' command button. Null/omitted auto-detects the CLI binary.

pi_button_visible.description: Show the built-in 'Pi' command button. Null/omitted auto-detects the CLI binary.

hermes_agent_button_visible.description: Show the built-in 'Hermes Agent' command button. Null/omitted auto-detects the CLI binary.

grok_button_visible.description: Show the built-in 'Grok' command button. Null/omitted auto-detects the CLI binary.

amp_button_visible.description: Show the built-in 'Amp' command button. Null/omitted auto-detects the CLI binary.

cursor_button_visible.description: Show the built-in 'Cursor' command button. Null/omitted auto-detects the CLI binary.

gemini_button_visible.description: Show the built-in 'Gemini' command button. Null/omitted auto-detects the CLI binary.

kiro_button_visible.description: Show the built-in 'Kiro' command button. Null/omitted auto-detects the CLI binary.

antigravity_button_visible.description: Show the built-in 'Antigravity' command button. Null/omitted auto-detects the CLI binary.

copilot_button_visible.description: Show the built-in 'Copilot' command button. Null/omitted auto-detects the CLI binary.

codebuddy_button_visible.description: Show the built-in 'CodeBuddy' command button. Null/omitted auto-detects the CLI binary.

factory_button_visible.description: Show the built-in 'Factory' command button. Null/omitted auto-detects the CLI binary.

qoder_button_visible.description: Show the built-in 'Qoder' command button. Null/omitted auto-detects the CLI binary.

openclaw_button_visible.description: Show the built-in 'Openclaw' command button. Null/omitted auto-detects the CLI binary.

agent_panel.description: Agents-view display, profile, and notification settings.

tool_permissions.description: Per-tool always-allow and always-deny input patterns.

telemetry.description: Opt-in desktop telemetry consent. The first-run consent modal only appears when 'enabled' is unanswered.

enabled.description: Tri-state consent: null = unanswered, true = opted in, false = opted out. The PANEFLOW_NO_TELEMETRY=1 environment variable overrides this unconditionally.

commandDefinition.description: Either a workspace definition (set 'workspace') or a shell command (set 'command'). The two fields are mutually exclusive.

description.description: Human-readable description shown in the command palette.

command.description: Shell command string. Mutually exclusive with 'workspace'.

cwd.description: Default working directory for the workspace.

color.description: Workspace accent color as a 6-digit lowercase hex string (no leading #).

ratio.description: Legacy single ratio for binary (2-child) layouts. Ignored when 'ratios' is present.

ratios.description: Per-child ratios for N-ary layouts. When present, must match children length and sum to ~1.0.

surface_type.description: Surface kind. Currently only 'terminal' is rendered.

name.description: Display name for the surface tab.

command.description: Shell command to run on surface creation.

cwd.description: Working directory override (relative paths resolve against the workspace cwd).

focus.description: When true, this surface receives initial focus.
